MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS AARON HULME and EUROFAHRT, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT:

Defendants Hulme and Eurofahrt, LLC's motion to strike paragraph 4 and 7 from the first amended complaint's (FAC) "Prayer for Relief" is denied. Corporations Code section 17706.02 specifically provides that a person may be "expelled as a member [of an LLC] by judicial order." (Corps. Code section 17706.02(e).) Section 17706.03 governs the effect of a person's resulting dissociation as a member: specifically that the dissociated person's right to participate as a member terminates, their fiduciary duties end, and any transferable interest owned before dissociation is owned by the person solely as a transferee-in other words, effectively a forfeiture of their voting, management and other membership interests, other than the limited right to receive distributions. (Corps. Code section 17706.03(a).) As discussed above in the demurrer, plaintiffs have leave to amend the eighth cause of action for judicial expulsion to allege acts or transactions that occurred after January 1, 2014. Accordingly, the court denies defendants' request to strike the prayer for relief at this time, or to edit its specific wording.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
